eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Open Market Committee held the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% following its April 28-29, 2026 meeting, reflecting policymaker caution amid core CPI inflation at 2.6% year-over-year in March—slightly softer than expected but still above the 2% goal—and resilient labor market conditions. Divergent views emerged, with four dissents and Boston Fed President Susan Collins signaling risks of higher-for-longer rates if inflation reaccelerates. CME FedWatch Tool shows near-100% odds of no change at the June 16-17 FOMC, with trader consensus implying modest easing later in 2026 if data softens. Traders should monitor May CPI, nonfarm payrolls, and June meeting for shifts in the market-implied rate path toward 2027.

markets.question: Will Trump speak to Elon Musk in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual speaks with Donald Trump between May 1 and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Speaking is defined as any verbal interaction between the listed individual and Donald Trump, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

The resolution source will be credible media reporting. However, in the absence of definitive or in the case of contradictory media reporting, statements by either of the specified individuals or their official representatives will also be considered.

Statements by the specified individuals or their official representatives will only be considered conclusive if the claims explicitly, or in context unambiguously, indicate that a qualifying talk occurred within the specified timeframe and took place via verbal communication, and provided that such claims are not contradicted by the other specified individual, their representatives, or credible media reporting by the end of the third calendar date (ET) following the statement in question.

If the date/time of a qualifying talk cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day following this market's above-specified timeframe, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether it was later confirmed to have taken place.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump's tie be blue on May 9, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on the predominant color of the tie worn by Donald Trump during his first public appearance during which he wears a tie on the specified date (ET).

The color will be determined based solely on the first publicly available photo or video of the appearance. Reporting of the tie color without photos or videos will not be considered. Subsequent publication of other photos or videos, including those taken earlier, will not affect the resolution.

For the tie to be considered predominantly blue or red, its primary color must be a shade of blue or red, though it may feature other colors as part of a pattern. 

This tie would count as "Blue":

If Donald Trump does not have any public appearance on the specified date, does not wear a tie, or if it is a color not listed among the strike options, the market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

events.title: NY-08 House Election Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries' unchallenged position as the Democratic nominee in solidly Democratic NY-08 (D+24 partisan voting index) underpins trader consensus favoring a Democratic House winner at 92%. Recent withdrawals of primary challengers, including councilmember Chi Osse after lacking key endorsements, leave Jeffries unopposed ahead of the June 23 closed primary, bolstered by $5.4 million cash on hand. Weak Republican contenders Lewis Mizrahi and Richard Simmons show no fundraising, aligning with Cook Political Report's Solid Democratic rating and Jeffries' past general election margins exceeding 75%. While a surprise GOP primary upset, personal scandal, or national Republican midterm surge could narrow odds, structural district advantages and incumbency present high barriers.

markets.question: Will Rizo Velovic win Survivor Season 50?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the contestant who wins Survivor season 50.

If Survivor season 50 concludes without a winner being declared, or if Survivor season 50 has otherwise not concluded by June 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET this market will resolve to "Other". In the case of a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed contestant whose name comes first in alphabetical order. 

This market will remain open until the conclusion of the season.

The resolution source for this market will be the official broadcast of the final episode of Survivor season 50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Aubry Bracco at 87.5% implied probability to win Survivor 50, fueled by spoiler leaks from fan communities that have accurately predicted every elimination since the February 2026 premiere, consistently positioning her as the frontrunner. Recent episodes, including Episode 11's "Everyone Will Be Shooketh!" aired early May, showcase her strategic scrappiness—gaining tribe trust on Day 16, orchestrating blindsides, and earning an overt winner's edit amid Cirie Fields' standout social game (7.1% odds). With the finale roughly two weeks away, upcoming immunity challenges and jury votes could affirm or upset this skin-in-the-game momentum, though historical late-game twists add uncertainty.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Chicago be between 48-49°F on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/il/chicago/KORD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service guidance for Chicago O'Hare International Airport, the official recording station, projects a high near 62-67°F on May 9, anchoring the 99.8% trader consensus for 56°F or higher amid strong model agreement from GFS and ECMWF ensembles. This reflects a synoptic setup featuring an upper-level ridge over the Midwest fostering warm air advection, light southerly winds, and mostly sunny to partly cloudy skies, consistent with May 9 climatological normals of 68°F after recent cooler trends. Inherent forecast uncertainty—typically ±4-6°F for 24-hour highs—leaves minimal room for sub-56°F outcomes, barring improbable developments like a sudden cold front surge or persistent morning stratus deck. Key updates include this afternoon's 18z model runs and initial hourly observations at dawn.

markets.question: Will Estonia be in the top 3 at Eurovision 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the named country finishes Eurovision 2026 as one of the top 3 highest scoring candidates.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to finish as one of the top 3 highest scorers based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: As first rehearsals wrap in Vienna's Wiener Stadthalle ahead of semis on May 12 and 14, trader consensus crowns Finland's "Liekinheitin" by Linda Lampenius & Pete Parkkonen as the top top-3 lockoff, propelled by its UMK national final triumph, jury-friendly rock energy, and OGAE poll dominance with 459 points last week. Greece's "Ferto" by Akylas surges on televote buzz from pre-parties, while Denmark's "Før vi går hjem" by Søren Torpegaard Lund holds firm via polished staging clips elevating its ballad appeal. Recent clips highlight Romania and Croatia dark horses, but historical Nordic strength and Big-5 qualifiers like France and Italy loom; second rehearsals and semi qualification runs this week could spark shifts before the May 16 final.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 99.9% implied probability to 29°C as the highest temperature in São Paulo on May 8, backed by official observations from the São Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport (SBGR) station on Weather Underground, which recorded a midday peak of 29°C amid partly cloudy skies and northerly winds at 10-20 km/h. This aligns with INMET's latest forecast guidance for highs in the 28-30°C range during autumn's mild conditions, where the diurnal heating cycle typically crests by early afternoon before cooling. Current late-day readings in the mid-20s°C, increasing cloud cover, and sunset approaching 17:35 local time further solidify this positioning. A challenge would require anomalous clear skies, subsidence, and radiative heating for a 1°C+ spike, but prevailing meteorological patterns render this highly unlikely.

markets.question: Will Marco Rubio win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

events.title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
events.description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the 2028 presidential election at 19.1%, reflecting his position as heir apparent in the Trump administration and strong CPAC straw poll support from March, though he awaits post-midterm clarity on a run. California Governor Gavin Newsom trails closely at 16.7% as the leading Democratic contender amid a thin bench, buoyed by national profile but hampered by negative favorability in recent Harvard polling. Secretary of State Marco Rubio's 14.9% surge stems from his "Hope for America" video sparking online momentum and GOP donor interest just days ago, plus positive +4 net favorability. This tight contest persists pre-primaries, hinging on November 2026 midterms, Trump signals, and favorability shifts; separation could arise from midterm gains in swing states or key endorsements.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Morgan Stanley as lead underwriter for SpaceX's anticipated 2026 IPO, with 49.5% implied probability, driven by the bank's longstanding ties to Elon Musk from prior deals like Tesla's IPO and recent reports positioning it as frontrunner amid a competitive banker selection process. Goldman Sachs (17.5%) and Bank of America (15.3%) follow closely, reflecting their prominence in January and April disclosures naming a core syndicate of four to five major banks—including JPMorgan—for the blockbuster Project Apex offering targeting $75 billion at up to $1.75 trillion valuation. A April 6 virtual meeting with 21 banks, led by Musk and CFO Bret Johnsen, solidified this lineup, though SpaceX plans alphabetical listing over traditional "lead left" designation, introducing uncertainty. Key catalysts ahead include the S-1 prospectus in late May and June roadshow.

markets.question: Will Zelenskyy post 140-159 posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Volodymyr Zelenskyy (@ZelenskyyUa), posts on X between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Zelenskyy's X posts from May 5-12 at 53% for 80-99 and 39% for 100-119, reflecting his established wartime pattern of 10+ daily updates amid the Russia-Ukraine conflict. Recent Russian strikes killing over 20 civilians just before May 9 Victory Day—despite Moscow's short-term ceasefire proposal—prompted Zelenskyy to intensify public condemnations and appeals for Western air defenses, sustaining elevated posting volumes seen in prior weeks. Frontline stability noted in early April has held without major shifts, but diplomatic maneuvering around Putin's parade truce and potential escalations through May 12 keep higher ranges viable, while extremes remain unlikely barring sudden de-escalation or crisis spikes.

markets.question: Will Auckland FC vs. Adelaide United FC end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 9, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Auckland FC vs. Adelaide United FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Australia A-League game, scheduled for Saturday, May 9, 2026 between Auckland FC and Adelaide United FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Auckland FC enters the A-League Men semi-final first leg as trader consensus slight favorite at 45.5% implied probability, buoyed by home advantage at Go Media Stadium and an unbeaten record in four prior high-scoring meetings with Adelaide United, including a recent 1-1 draw marred by nine yellows and a goalkeeper injury swap. Auckland advanced past Melbourne City via penalties last weekend despite ongoing injury concerns sidelining Felipe Gallegos and Marlee François, though Francis de Vries returns from a calf strain. Adelaide, second in the regular-season table with 43 points to Auckland's 42, boasts stronger recent form but faces a blow without suspended joint Golden Boot winner Luka Jovanovic, tempering their 28.5% away win odds while a draw at 26.5% reflects the physical, unpredictable rivalry.

eventMetadata.context_description: Austria holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 43% implied probability for victory over Algeria (37.5%) in this pivotal 2026 FIFA World Cup Group J finale at neutral Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City, with a draw at 29.5% underscoring the tight contest between evenly ranked sides (Austria 24th, Algeria 28th FIFA). Algeria's mounting injury crisis—goalkeepers Anthony Mandrea (shoulder surgery) and Melvin Mastil (groin hernia) sidelined, plus Yacine Titraoui (hamstring) and Amine Gouiri (muscle)—has eroded squad depth just weeks from kickoff, tempering their strong African qualifying campaign. Austria, buoyed by recent friendlies wins over Ghana (5-1) and South Korea (1-0) in late March, leverages superior recent form and defensive solidity despite their own concerns like Maximilian Wöber's muscular issues, keeping probabilities bunched in this high-stakes neutral-venue showdown absent recent head-to-head history.

Verona's injury crisis deepens ahead of this Serie A round 36 home clash, sidelining forwards Daniel Mosquera (meniscus tear) and Cheikh Niasse (physical discomfort), defender Daniel Oyegoke (ankle), and Armel Bella-Kotchap (shoulder), despite their gritty 1-1 draw at Juventus last weekend. Como, managed by Cesc Fàbregas, boasts solid recent away form including a 0-0 at Napoli and 2-0 at Genoa, plus a 2-0-1 head-to-head edge over Verona, fueling the lopsided market sentiment.

markets.question: Will Liverpool finish in the top 4 of the EPL 2025–26 standings?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs finish in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings for the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club officially finishes in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings at the end of the season, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If two or more clubs are tied on points for a top 4 position, resolution will follow the official English Premier League tiebreaking procedures.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed before October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal (76 points from 35 games) and Manchester City (71 points from 34, with a game in hand) have effectively secured Champions League qualification through top-4 finishes, while Manchester United hold third place on 64 points from 35 games, six clear of the pack. The fierce battle for fourth pits Liverpool (58 points, +12 GD) against Aston Villa (58 points, +4 GD) after both played 35 matches, with Liverpool's superior goal difference providing a slight edge. Matchweek 36 looms critically: Liverpool host Chelsea on Saturday, Villa travel to Burnley, United face Sunderland away, and Arsenal visit West Ham. United captain Bruno Fernandes' recent Football Writers' Association Footballer of the Year award highlights their momentum amid a congested title and European race.

markets.question: Will Solana dip to $20 by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to “Yes” if any Binance 1-minute candle for Solana (SOL/USDT) between November 24, 2025, 14:30 and December 31, 2026, 23:59 in the ET timezone has a final “Low” price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT “Low” prices available at:
https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT with the chart settings on “1m” (one-minute candles) selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance SOL/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

markets.question: Will "Pinocchio" be said on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
Drake is expected to release his new album "ICEMAN" in 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone on the specified transcript for any track on the officially released full "ICEMAN" album. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Drake's ninth studio album ICEMAN arrives May 15 via OVO Sound and Republic Records, capping a cryptic rollout with Toronto ice sculptures melted by fans to reveal the date, cemetery visuals, and a virtual mansion "icing." markets.question: Israel x Lebanon diplomatic meeting by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of Israel and Lebanon by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding Israel-Lebanon relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the Israel and Lebanon, and a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no in-person Trump-Putin meeting by June 30, reflecting the absence of any scheduled bilateral summit amid ongoing Ukraine diplomacy conducted primarily through phone calls and envoys. President Trump's May 8 announcement of a three-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire, including a 1,000-prisoner swap around Victory Day, followed a 1.5-hour April 29 call where Putin floated a truce and Trump described a conflict resolution deal as "imminent," yet no face-to-face venue was set. Earlier U.S. invitation to Putin for the December G20 in Miami underscores distant horizons, with Kremlin statements confirming no near-term plans. Prior 2025 summits in Alaska and floated Budapest talks contrast current virtual engagement; a sudden escalation signal, breakthrough in peace negotiations, or neutral-site offer like Turkey or Switzerland could prompt an abrupt shift.

markets.question: Will T1 make a roster change before July?

For the purpose of this market, "roster change" refers to any official signing, transfer, benching to inactive/reserve, release, retirement, or departure of a player from the active lineup. This includes adding new players (transfers, free agent signings, or loans), removing players (to inactive, free agency, or other teams), or any change resulting in a different active roster.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no charges against Les Wexner by June 30, reflecting the absence of any DOJ indictment or federal prosecution despite heightened scrutiny from recently released Epstein files and a February 18, 2026, House Oversight Committee deposition. In the nearly five-hour testimony, the billionaire retail magnate denied knowledge of Jeffrey Epstein's crimes, claimed he was "duped" by his former financial advisor, and affirmed cutting ties nearly two decades ago after discovering theft—assertions uncontradicted by criminal action to date. A civil lawsuit filed in early April alleging financial support for Epstein's activities has not prompted charges, underscoring procedural hurdles like statutes of limitations on older claims. With no active federal probes announced and resolution looming in seven weeks, traders price slim odds of sudden developments like new victim evidence triggering an indictment.

markets.question: Noah Okafor: Anytime Goalscorer
markets.description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Tottenham Hotspur FC and Leeds United FC, scheduled for May 11, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Noah Okafor is credited with a goal in the official box score within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. eventMetadata.context_description: With Tottenham Hotspur (17th, 37 points from 35 matches) hosting Leeds United (14th, 43 points) in a critical Premier League relegation showdown on May 11 at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, trader consensus reflects Spurs' 55% implied win probability amid home advantage despite their injury crisis. Tottenham remain depleted without striker Dominic Solanke, centre-back Cristian Romero, goalkeeper Guglielmo Vicario, and wingers Mohammed Kudus and Wilson Odobert, but recent returns of James Maddison and Rodrigo Bentancur enhance midfield output. Leeds lack defender Joe Rodon and midfielder Anton Stach (ankle injuries), following wins over Burnley (3-1) and Wolves (2-0). Player props for shots, assists, and goals from Son Heung-min, Maddison, or Leeds' Wilfried Gnonto face uncertainty from Spurs' poor home form (2 wins) and mutual defensive frailties.

markets.question: Will Oliver Bearman get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the driver who achieves pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 23, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the driver who is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a driver sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that driver.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a dead-even field for pole position at the Canadian Grand Prix, with all 22 drivers priced at 50% implied probability amid 2026's transformative active aero and sustainable power unit regulations that have compressed one-lap pace across the grid. Early-season qualifying has featured razor-thin margins, highlighted by rookie sensation Kimi Antonelli's perfect 100% win rate from pole in limited starts and strong average finishes near the front, alongside consistent top-end showings from Oscar Piastri, Max Verstappen, and George Russell. Circuit Gilles Villeneuve's low-downforce demands for straight-line speed and braking stability favor versatile setups, but with no practice sessions yet and over three weeks until the May 22-24 weekend, no clear qualifying favorite has emerged to shift sentiment.

markets.question: Will Lars Boje Mathiesen be the next prime minister of Denmark after the 2026 parliamentary elections?

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed as Prime Minister by the Danish monarch. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

However, prolonged coalition negotiations have deadlocked, culminating in a May 8 breakdown when a key partner withdrew, prompting the king to task Venstre leader and Defence Minister Troels Lund Poulsen (12.2%) with exploring a centre-right government—elevating his position as a consensus figure. Lars Løkke Rasmussen (5.3%, Moderates) remains a potential kingmaker in cross-bloc talks, with outcomes hinging on upcoming negotiations amid policy sticking points like defence and Greenland.

markets.question: Will no company have an AI model hit 1550 on Chatbot Arena in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed entity, which is the first to reach an Arena Score of 1550+ on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

Results from the "Text Arena" section on the leaderboard/text tab of https://lmarena.ai/ with the style control unchecked (https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/overall-no-style-control) will be used to resolve this market.

If no company's model reaches 1550+ Arena Score by the specified time, this market will resolve to "None in 2026".

If the first model to reach a 1550+ Arena Score belongs to any company other than the listed ones, this market will be resolved to "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source becomes unavailable, the market will remain open until it is accessible again. If it becomes permanently unavailable, resolution will be based on another credible source.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Moscow be 10°C on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by NOAA at the Vnukovo International Airport in degrees Celsius on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=UUWW

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

This follows warnings from Russia's Phobos weather center on May 7 about Arctic air masses advancing over European Russia starting May 8, bringing cloudy skies, northerly winds, and possible light precipitation that suppress daytime heating. Recent observations show May 8 highs struggling around 11-15°C with rain, well below the typical early May climatological average of 18°C. New forecast updates overnight could refine this, as model ensembles carry uncertainty in exact peak temperatures within the 11-13°C cluster.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pakistan Meteorological Department forecasts indicate Karachi's maximum temperature on May 9 will likely range 33–35°C under continued warm, humid conditions with west/southwesterly winds, following May 8's observed peak of 34°C after an early-month heatwave that hit 44°C. Trader sentiment splits evenly between 34°C and 35°C due to uncertainty in Arabian Sea breeze strength—potentially capping peaks via evaporative cooling and marine air advection if winds exceed 15 km/h—or allowing continental heating under the persistent high-pressure ridge to nudge higher. Elevated humidity (55–65%) may temper extremes, with models showing 1°C spreads; PMD's daily update today could clarify before airport station resolution. Historical May averages hover near 35°C amid pre-monsoon subsidence.

markets.question: Will LeBron James buy the Seattle Seahawks?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the named individual who is publicly announced as having agreed to purchase the Seattle Seahawks by September 9, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET (the beginning of the 2026 NFL regular season). 

If no sale is announced before this time, this market will resolve to "Other".

For the purposes of this market, an announcement must confirm that the Paul G. Allen estate (also known as Vulcan LLC) has reached a binding agreement to sell a majority interest in the team. Minority sales will not be considered.

If a sale is announced to an individual that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If two or more listed individuals are announced as joint owners or part of the same ownership group, this market will resolve to the individual announced as the controlling owner.

If two or more listed individuals are announced as the controlling owners, this market will resolve to the individual whose last name comes first alphabetically.

For the purposes of this market, the completion (closing) of the sale is not required.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the National Football League or the Seattle Seahawks. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the median home value in the DC Metro area be between $566,000 and $572,000 on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the median home value for all property types in the Washington, D.C. Metro area on May 31, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source will be official data from the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for the Washington, D.C. Metro area (Parcl_ID: 2900475). The settlement price will be calculated by multiplying the published price index value (price per square foot) by 1800 square feet, which is the median home size in the Washington, D.C. Metro area. Parcl is set to publish this data on May 31, 2026. If no data for May 31 is released by June 10, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve according to the most recently published data. (see: https://app.parcllabs.com/prediction-market-resolutions/36)

markets.question: Set 1 Winner: Sabalenka vs Cirstea
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Aryna Sabalenka and Sorana Cirstea in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 9, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to “Sabalenka” if Aryna Sabalenka wins the first set. It will resolve to “Cirstea” if Sorana Cirstea wins the first set.
If the match begins but is not completed, and the first set is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on that completed set. If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA match statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Aryna Sabalenka vs Sorana Cirstea Set 1 Winner

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Aryna Sabalenka vs Sorana Cirstea
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Aryna Sabalenka and Sorana Cirstea in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 9, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Aryna Sabalenka' if Aryna Sabalenka advances against Sorana Cirstea.

This market will resolve to 'Sorana Cirstea' if Sorana Cirstea advances against Aryna Sabalenka.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka enters as a heavy favorite at the Internazionali BNL d'Italia after a dominant 6-2, 6-3 second-round win over Barbora Krejcikova on clay, showcasing her explosive baseline power and improved movement on the surface following a strong Madrid run marred only by a quarterfinal loss to Hailey Baptiste. To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tight clustering of market-implied probabilities around 27–29°C reflects the Central Weather Administration's (CWA) latest forecast for Taipei's May 10 high of up to 28°C under persistent cloudy skies, following cool recent days with May 8 at 24–27°C and May 9 at 21–23°C due to Meiyu-season showers and southerly moisture flows. Differentiating 28°C as the trader favorite are ensemble projections from ECMWF and GFS models indicating potential afternoon partial clearing to enhance diurnal heating and urban heat island amplification in the Taipei basin, versus 27°C if overcast suppresses solar insolation or late showers cool surface temperatures. Updated CWA observations and model runs expected overnight could shift the balance ahead of official station resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors a 27°C high in Tel Aviv on May 9 at 46% implied probability, driven by the latest Israel Meteorological Service (IMS) guidance and ECMWF/GFS model ensembles projecting peaks of 26–28°C under a stable high-pressure ridge promoting subsidence and clear skies over the eastern Mediterranean. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the PL at 81.5% implied probability to win the most seats in Brazil's October 4, 2026, Senate election, where 54 positions are up for renewal, driven by the party's strong showings in recent state-level polls and aggressive candidate launches. Late April-early May Datatrends, Verita, and Simetria surveys highlight PL contenders like André do Prado in São Paulo—endorsed by Eduardo Bolsonaro and predicted by Governor Tarcísio de Freitas to lead votes—and competitive positioning in Bahia, Pará, and the Midwest, building on PL's current status as the Senate's largest party. The MDB (5.1%) benefits from centrists like Helder Barbalho but lags in projections; UNIÃO and PSD trail amid fragmented opposition. Senate rejection of Lula's Supreme Court nominee on April 29 further energized right-wing bets, though coalition negotiations and fresh polls ahead of primaries remain pivotal.

eventMetadata.context_description: Adam Hamilton's late entry into the Kansas Democratic U.S. Senate primary on April 30 has propelled him to overwhelming trader consensus at 77.5%, fueled by his rapid $1 million fundraising haul announced May 4 and high name recognition as pastor of the nation's largest United Methodist church in Leawood. This momentum has overshadowed state Sen. Patrick Schmidt, previously the Q1 fundraising leader now at 11.4%, in a crowded field of over 10 candidates lacking public polls. Sandy Spidel Neumann holds third at 4.8% amid minimal shifts. With the August 4 primary approaching, traders weigh early momentum and moderate appeal in a deep-red state where Democrats seek a viable general election challenger to incumbent Sen. Roger Marshall.

markets.question: Stephon Castle: Rebounds O/U 5.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 8 at 9:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Stephon Castle records more than 5.5 total rebounds during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Stephon Castle records 5.5 rebounds or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

events.title: Spurs vs. Timberwolves
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 8 at 12:00AM ET:
If the Spurs win, the market will resolve to "Spurs".
If the Timberwolves win, the market will resolve to "Timberwolves".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: In Game 3 of the Western Conference semifinals at Target Center, the series-tied 1-1 Spurs hold trader consensus as road favorites amid Minnesota's injury concerns, with Anthony Edwards questionable due to a left knee bone bruise sustained earlier in the playoffs and Ayo Dosunmu questionable with a right calf issue, while Donte DiVincenzo remains out with an Achilles injury. San Antonio, fully healthy with Victor Wembanyama leading after a recent concussion recovery, dominated Game 2 with a 133-95 rout fueled by 34-8 transition edge, leveraging their league-best 62-20 regular-season record against the 49-33 Timberwolves. Home-court advantage and potential Edwards return could spark a Wolves rebound, but San Antonio's defensive pressure and momentum define the closely contested implied probabilities.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-21 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-21 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: The open NY-21 House seat, vacated by Rep. Elise Stefanik's December 2025 decision to end her gubernatorial bid and retire from Congress, drives trader consensus toward a Republican hold at 65.5% implied probability, reflecting the district's deep-red upstate New York North Country profile where Donald Trump won overwhelmingly in 2024. Recent dueling polls from early May underscore a competitive GOP primary led by Anthony Constantino, whose lead widened after President Trump's April 21 endorsement bucked the state party's March backing of Robert Smullen, ahead of the June 23 primaries. Democrats field weaker challengers with no standout polling, aligning with historical R+12 partisan lean and incumbency-free Republican advantages in safe seats.

markets.question: Will Arvid Lindblad be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli's commanding championship lead after victory at the 2026 Miami Grand Prix on May 3—his third win in six rounds—has propelled him to 38.8% implied probability as traders' consensus favorite, with Mercedes' dominant car enabling consistent podiums and 100 points atop the drivers' standings. Teammate George Russell sits second at 80 points and 29.5% odds, buoyed by his Australian GP win but trailing after recent runner-up finishes amid intra-team pressure. Lando Norris (10.9%) gains from McLaren's pace but lags in points, while Max Verstappen's 6.3% reflects Red Bull's struggles; the field trails as Mercedes' tire strategy and race pace advantages solidify the intra-squad title battle early in the season.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat George Latimer holds a commanding position in New York's 16th Congressional District, a Cook PVI D+18 stronghold where Kamala Harris won 66% in the 2024 presidential race, reflecting trader consensus at 94.5% for the Democratic Party ahead of the November 3 general election. Latimer's 71% landslide victory over Republican Miriam Flisser in 2024, coupled with superior fundraising ($134,000 cash on hand as of late March versus zero for GOP presumptive nominee Joseph Cinquemani), solidifies the race as Solid Democratic per Cook Political Report, Inside Elections, and Sabato's Crystal Ball. With no Democratic primary challengers filed by the April deadline and weak GOP opposition, markets price in minimal upset risk. Potential shifts could arise from a late scandal, health issue for Latimer, or extraordinary Republican national midterm tailwinds, though historical base rates for such safe seats favor continuity.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Janelle Bynum's bid for reelection in Oregon's 5th Congressional District has solidified trader consensus at 82.5% for the Democratic Party, bolstered by her strong fundraising, endorsements from EMILYs List and pro-Israel groups, and a district that delivered solid support for Kamala Harris in 2024. Recent Cook Political Report upgrades to Likely Democrat reflect Republicans' recruitment struggles, with Deschutes County Commissioner Patti Adair and challenger Jonathan Lockwood as the leading GOP primary contenders lacking broader appeal in this former battleground. The May 19 primary looms as the next catalyst, but historical incumbency advantages and weak opposition sustain high Democratic implied probabilities amid limited polling.

events.title: Korea Republic vs. Czechia
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Thursday, June 11, 2026 between Korea Republic and Czechia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Korea Republic a slim 34.5% favorite over Czechia at 34.0% with draw at 31.0%, capturing the razor-thin dynamics of this FIFA World Cup Group A opener at neutral Estadio Akron in high-altitude Guadalajara. Czechia's momentum from their March playoff triumph over Denmark via penalties contrasts South Korea's back-to-back friendly losses to Ivory Coast and Austria, fostering uncertainty despite Korea's higher 25th FIFA ranking versus Czechia's 41st. Limited head-to-head history leans toward draws, while potential absences like South Korea's Hwang In-beom and Jae-Sung Lee alongside Czechia's Ladislav Krejcí add volatility to lineups heavy on stars like Son Heung-min and Tomas Soucek. Both sides eye crucial early points against a Mexico-led group.

markets.question: Will the price of Solana be less than $40 on May 10?

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

markets.question: Will Partido dos Trabalhadores (PT) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

events.title: Next Brazil Senate Election: Most Seats Won
events.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Brazilian Senate, not by any federation or coalition it may be a part of.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the PL at 81.5% implied probability to win the most seats in Brazil's October 4, 2026, Senate election, where 54 positions are up for renewal, driven by the party's strong showings in recent state-level polls and aggressive candidate launches. Late April-early May Datatrends, Verita, and Simetria surveys highlight PL contenders like André do Prado in São Paulo—endorsed by Eduardo Bolsonaro and predicted by Governor Tarcísio de Freitas to lead votes—and competitive positioning in Bahia, Pará, and the Midwest, building on PL's current status as the Senate's largest party. The MDB (5.1%) benefits from centrists like Helder Barbalho but lags in projections; UNIÃO and PSD trail amid fragmented opposition. Senate rejection of Lula's Supreme Court nominee on April 29 further energized right-wing bets, though coalition negotiations and fresh polls ahead of primaries remain pivotal.
